evals.name: build-rdf-graph
evals.prompt: Write a Python function that builds an RDF graph from ontology data. The ontology has id 'onto-001', name 'NBC 2025', base_iri 'https://onto-aec.ca/nbc/'. Add at least 3 concepts with labels and definitions. Use rdflib with proper namespace bindings.
evals.expected_output: A function that creates an rdflib.Graph with ontology metadata and concept nodes
expectations: The function creates a new rdflib.Graph
Namespaces are bound using g.bind()
OWL.Ontology type is added to the ontology URI
At least 3 concepts are added with RDF.type OWL.Class
Concepts have RDFS.label and RDFS.comment for definitions

evals.name: add-subclass-relationships
evals.prompt: Write a function that adds rdfs:subClassOf relationships between concepts in an RDF graph. Given lists of from_concept_id and to_concept_id, add the appropriate triples.
evals.expected_output: A function that adds subclass relationships to an existing graph
expectations: Uses RDFS.subClassOf predicate
Creates URIRef for each concept using the namespace
Adds triples correctly to the graph
Returns the updated graph

evals.name: shacl-validation
evals.prompt: Write a function that validates an RDF graph using SHACL. It should return whether the graph conforms and list any violations with their severity and message.
evals.expected_output: A function that returns validation results with conform status and violations
expectations: Uses pyshacl.validate() or run_shacl_validation()
Returns conforms boolean
Parses violations with severity (ERROR/WARNING/INFO)
Includes violation messages in the return

evals.name: serialize-turtle-format
evals.prompt: Write a function that serializes an RDF graph to Turtle format. It should handle the serialization and return the turtle string.
evals.expected_output: A function that serializes graph to Turtle format
expectations: Uses g.serialize(format='turtle')
Returns the serialized string
Handles potential serialization errors

evals.name: standards-alignment
evals.prompt: Write a function that adds SKOS alignment relations between an ontology concept and an IFC/OmniClass standard. Use exactMatch for high-confidence alignments.
evals.expected_output: A function that adds standards alignment triples to a graph
expectations: Uses SKOS.exactMatch predicate
Creates proper URIRefs for both concept and standard
Can handle different match types (exact, close, broad, narrow)
Adds confidence as a literal if needed
